title: Stages of Labor: A Systematic Approach
objectives: Understand the pathophysiology of Stages of Labor
Identify key diagnostic features of Stages of Labor
Apply clinical reasoning to Stages of Labor management
Recognize common pitfalls in Stages of Labor

sections.title: Overview of Stages of Labor
sections.content:
**Stages of Labor**



**Key Points:**
• Stage 1 Latent: onset of regular contractions to 6cm dilation; can be prolonged (>20h nullip, >14h multip)
• Stage 1 Active: 6cm to complete dilation (10cm); expected rate ~1cm/hr
• Stage 2: complete dilation to delivery; <3h nullip, <2h multip (with epidural add 1h)

sections.title: Pathophysiology & Key Features
sections.content:
• Stage 1 Latent: onset of regular contractions to 6cm dilation; can be prolonged (>20h nullip, >14h multip)
• Stage 1 Active: 6cm to complete dilation (10cm); expected rate ~1cm/hr

sections.title: Diagnosis & Management
sections.content:
• Stage 2: complete dilation to delivery; <3h nullip, <2h multip (with epidural add 1h)
• Stage 3: delivery of infant to delivery of placenta; normally <30 minutes
• Arrest of active labor: no cervical change for ≥6h with adequate contractions → consider cesarean

sections.title: Clinical Pearls
sections.content:
**High-Yield Points for Stages of Labor:**

• Stage 2: complete dilation to delivery; <3h nullip, <2h multip (with epidural add 1h)
• Stage 3: delivery of infant to delivery of placenta; normally <30 minutes
• Arrest of active labor: no cervical change for ≥6h with adequate contractions → consider cesarean

mcqs.question: Which of the following is MOST characteristic of Stages of Labor?

options.text: Stage 1 Latent: onset of regular contractions to 6cm dilation; can be prolonged (>20h nullip, >14h multip)
options.explanation: Stage 1 Latent: onset of regular contractions to 6cm dilation; can be prolonged (>20h nullip, >14h multip)

options.text: Requires invasive testing in all cases

mcqs.question: What is the KEY diagnostic or management consideration for Stages of Labor?

options.text: Arrest of active labor: no cervical change for ≥6h with adequate contractions → consider cesarean
options.explanation: Arrest of active labor: no cervical change for ≥6h with adequate contractions → consider cesarean

options.text: Immediate surgical intervention in all cases

mcqs.question: A patient presents with findings consistent with Stages of Labor. Which finding would you expect?

options.text: Stage 1 Active: 6cm to complete dilation (10cm); expected rate ~1cm/hr
options.explanation: Stage 1 Active: 6cm to complete dilation (10cm); expected rate ~1cm/hr

mcqs.stem: A G2P1 at 38 weeks presents with painless bright red vaginal bleeding. Ultrasound shows a complete placenta previa. What is the recommended management?

options.text: Scheduled cesarean delivery at 36-37 weeks if stable; emergent C-section if hemorrhaging
options.explanation: Per ACOG, complete placenta previa is an absolute contraindication to vaginal delivery due to risk of life-threatening hemorrhage. If asymptomatic, planned cesarean at 36-37+6 weeks is recommended. Active hemorrhage at any gestational age requires emergent cesarean. At 38 weeks with bleeding, proceed to delivery.

options.text: Attempt vaginal delivery with close monitoring
options.explanation: Vaginal delivery is absolutely contraindicated with complete placenta previa — the placenta completely covers the cervical os, and cervical dilation would cause catastrophic hemorrhage.

options.text: Expectant management with bed rest until 40 weeks
options.explanation: At 38 weeks with active bleeding and complete previa, expectant management risks maternal hemorrhage. Delivery is indicated.

options.text: Digital cervical exam to assess dilation
options.explanation: Digital cervical exam is CONTRAINDICATED in placenta previa — it can disrupt the placenta and precipitate massive hemorrhage. Always confirm placental location by ultrasound before any cervical examination in third-trimester bleeding.
